story.iWant: Swagger/OpenAPI documentation configured in the application
story.soThat: API endpoints are automatically documented and testable via Swagger UI

subtasks.subtask: Check package.json for @nestjs/swagger@^11.2.1
Verify package compatibility with NestJS v11.x
Run npm install if package missing (unlikely)
Open nest-cli.json file
Add compilerOptions.plugins array if not present
Add @nestjs/swagger plugin with classValidatorShim and introspectComments options
Verify plugin configuration syntax
Import SwaggerModule and DocumentBuilder from @nestjs/swagger
Add SWAGGER_ENABLED environment variable check
Create DocumentBuilder with title, description, version, and Bearer auth
Generate OpenAPI document with SwaggerModule.createDocument
Setup Swagger UI with SwaggerModule.setup at /api/docs
Wrap Swagger setup in try-catch for graceful error handling
Test configuration with development environment
Add SWAGGER_ENABLED=true to .env.example with documentation
Document expected values: true/false
Document default behavior (enabled in dev, disabled in prod)
Import Logger from @nestjs/common
Log when Swagger enabled: 'Swagger UI available at /api/docs'
Log when Swagger disabled: 'Swagger disabled'

task.description: Verify CORS configuration for Swagger UI

subtasks.subtask: Check existing CORS configuration in main.ts
Ensure CORS allows /api/docs routes
Test Swagger UI loads without CORS errors
Test "Try it out" functionality with JWT token
Start application with SWAGGER_ENABLED=true and verify /api/docs loads
Verify Swagger UI displays title, description, version, and Authorize button
Test OpenAPI JSON export at /api/docs-json
Restart with SWAGGER_ENABLED=false and verify 404 responses
Verify application logs Swagger status correctly

task.description: Write integration tests for Swagger setup

subtasks.subtask: Create test/swagger-setup.e2e-spec.ts
Test GET /api/docs returns 200 and contains 'swagger-ui' when enabled
Test GET /api/docs-json returns valid OpenAPI JSON with correct metadata
Test both endpoints return 404 when SWAGGER_ENABLED=false

criterion.details: - Package compatible with NestJS v11.x - Already installed in project (verified in package.json) - Swagger module provides DocumentBuilder and SwaggerModule

criterion.description: Swagger configured in main.ts with DocumentBuilder
criterion.details: - Swagger setup executes during application bootstrap - Configuration includes: Title "Boilerplate API", Description "Production-ready NestJS Boilerplate API", Version "1.0" - Bearer JWT authentication configured with addBearerAuth() - Authentication scheme: Bearer JWT with name "JWT-auth"

- SwaggerModule.setup('api/docs', app, document) configured - Swagger UI loads successfully in browser - Load time < 2 seconds (performance requirement) - UI displays configured title, description, and version - "Authorize" button visible for JWT token input

- SWAGGER_ENABLED environment variable controls Swagger activation - When SWAGGER_ENABLED=true: Swagger UI accessible at /api/docs - When SWAGGER_ENABLED=false: /api/docs returns 404 Not Found - Default: true in development/staging, false in production - Environment variable documented in .env.example

- Endpoint returns complete OpenAPI 3.0 specification - JSON format with correct content-type header - Document includes all configured metadata (title, version, auth) - Accessible when SWAGGER_ENABLED=true, returns 404 when false

- Plugin: @nestjs/swagger with options - classValidatorShim: true (auto-infer from class-validator decorators) - introspectComments: true (extract JSDoc comments as descriptions) - Plugin enables compile-time metadata extraction - Reduces runtime overhead for Swagger documentation

- Log message: "Swagger UI available at /api/docs" when enabled - Log message: "Swagger disabled" when SWAGGER_ENABLED=false - Logs include Swagger UI URL for developer convenience - Graceful error handling: Swagger errors don't crash application

criterion.description: CORS configuration allows Swagger UI access
criterion.details: - CORS enabled for /api/docs routes - Swagger UI assets load successfully (no CORS errors) - API requests from Swagger UI succeed - "Try it out" functionality operational

doc.title: Epic 8 Technical Specification: API Documentation (Swagger)
doc.snippet: Story 8.1 implements Swagger module setup: install @nestjs/swagger, configure in main.ts with DocumentBuilder (title, description, version, Bearer auth), setup at /api/docs, environment-controlled via SWAGGER_ENABLED, JSON export at /api/docs-json. Plugin configured in nest-cli.json for compile-time metadata extraction.

doc.title: Epic 8 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Swagger UI load time: < 2 seconds. OpenAPI document generation: < 500ms. Plugin overhead: < 50ms per request. Memory footprint: < 100MB additional. Zero overhead when SWAGGER_ENABLED=false.

doc.title: Epic 8 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Production Swagger access controlled via SWAGGER_ENABLED=false (default). Bearer JWT auth configured. Sensitive data exposure prevention with @Exclude() decorators. CORS configuration for Swagger UI access.

doc.title: Epic 8 Technical Specification
doc.section: Dependencies and Integrations - nest-cli.json Plugin Configuration
doc.snippet: Plugin configuration: @nestjs/swagger with classValidatorShim: true (auto-infer from class-validator decorators) and introspectComments: true (extract JSDoc comments). Compile-time metadata extraction reduces runtime overhead.

doc.title: Swagger Implementation Pattern
doc.snippet: Layer 1: Plugin (automatic DTO documentation). Layer 2: ApiEndpoint decorator (unified endpoint docs). Layer 3: CRUD shortcuts (@ApiGetAll, @ApiGetOne, @ApiCreate, @ApiUpdate, @ApiDelete). Minimal code, maximum documentation.

doc.title: Project Structure
doc.snippet: main.ts is the application bootstrap file. Current setup: Sentry initialization, logger configuration, global interceptors (LoggingInterceptor), global filters (SentryExceptionFilter), validation pipe, Prisma graceful shutdown. Swagger configuration will be added here.

doc.title: Implementation Patterns
doc.snippet: Wrap configuration in try-catch blocks. Log errors with clear context. Don't let optional features crash the application. Graceful degradation for non-critical features.

doc.title: Story 7.6: Health Check Endpoints
doc.snippet: Application bootstrap pattern: main.ts is central config file. Configuration order matters: CORS → Swagger → Interceptors → Pipes. Use Logger for startup messages. Environment variable management: process.env and .env.example. Error handling: wrap in try-catch, graceful degradation.

artifact.reason: Main application bootstrap file where Swagger configuration will be added. Currently initializes Sentry, logger, interceptors, filters, validation pipe. Swagger setup follows same pattern.
NestJS CLI configuration where Swagger plugin will be added. Currently has i18n assets configuration. Plugin array needs to be added to compilerOptions.
Environment variable template where SWAGGER_ENABLED variable will be documented. Used as reference for all required environment variables.
Winston logger service from Story 7.3. Used for logging Swagger initialization status in bootstrap function.

node.package: Core Swagger/OpenAPI integration for NestJS. Provides DocumentBuilder and SwaggerModule.
NestJS decorators and utilities. Provides Logger for startup messages.
NestJS core framework. Provides NestFactory for application creation.

constraint.description: Swagger configuration must be added to main.ts bootstrap function
constraint.rationale: main.ts is the central application bootstrap file. Swagger setup follows existing pattern: Sentry → Logger → Interceptors → Filters → Validation → Swagger.

constraint.description: Swagger must be environment-controlled via SWAGGER_ENABLED variable
constraint.rationale: Security requirement: Swagger disabled by default in production. Development/staging enabled for API testing. Prevents sensitive endpoint exposure.

constraint.description: Swagger setup errors must not crash the application
constraint.rationale: Graceful degradation pattern: wrap Swagger configuration in try-catch, log errors, allow application to continue. Swagger is non-critical feature.

constraint.description: Swagger UI load time < 2 seconds, document generation < 500ms
constraint.rationale: Developer experience requirement. Plugin provides compile-time optimization. Lazy document generation with caching.

constraint.description: Plugin must be configured in nest-cli.json with classValidatorShim and introspectComments
constraint.rationale: Compile-time metadata extraction reduces runtime overhead. Auto-infers types from class-validator decorators and JSDoc comments.

constraint.description: Log Swagger status on startup using LoggerService
constraint.rationale: Visibility into application configuration. Log "Swagger UI available at /api/docs" when enabled, "Swagger disabled" when disabled.

interface.name: DocumentBuilder
interface.usage: Configure OpenAPI document metadata: title, description, version, authentication scheme.

interface.name: SwaggerModule.createDocument
interface.usage: Generate OpenAPI document from NestJS application and configuration. Returns document for Swagger UI.

interface.name: SwaggerModule.setup
interface.signature: SwaggerModule.setup(path: string, app: INestApplication, document: OpenAPIDocument): void
interface.usage: Mount Swagger UI at specified path (e.g., 'api/docs'). Serves OpenAPI documentation with interactive testing interface.

interface.name: LoggerService
interface.usage: Winston logger service from Story 7.3. Use for logging Swagger initialization status with context.

interface.name: process.env.SWAGGER_ENABLED
interface.usage: Controls Swagger activation. Check with: if (process.env.SWAGGER_ENABLED === 'true'). Document in .env.example.

tests.standards: E2E testing for Swagger setup using Jest and supertest. Test both enabled and disabled states. Verify Swagger UI accessibility at /api/docs and OpenAPI JSON export at /api/docs-json. Test response structure (HTML contains 'swagger-ui', JSON matches OpenAPI 3.0 spec). Verify 404 responses when SWAGGER_ENABLED=false. Test file location: test/swagger-setup.e2e-spec.ts. Coverage target: Critical paths (enabled/disabled scenarios).

ideas.idea: Test @nestjs/swagger package is installed by importing SwaggerModule and DocumentBuilder without errors.
Test DocumentBuilder configuration: verify title, description, version are set correctly in generated OpenAPI JSON.
Test GET /api/docs returns 200 OK when SWAGGER_ENABLED=true and response HTML contains 'swagger-ui' string.
Test SWAGGER_ENABLED=false: both /api/docs and /api/docs-json return 404 Not Found.
Test GET /api/docs-json returns valid JSON with OpenAPI 3.0 structure, includes title "Boilerplate API", version "1.0".
Test OpenAPI JSON includes security scheme "JWT-auth" with Bearer authentication configuration.
Test nest-cli.json contains plugins array with @nestjs/swagger configuration (build-time verification).
Test application logs contain "Swagger UI available at /api/docs" or "Swagger disabled" based on SWAGGER_ENABLED value.
Test CORS: Swagger UI assets load successfully, API requests from Swagger UI succeed (verify with Try-it-out functionality).
Performance test: Swagger UI first load completes in < 2 seconds, OpenAPI document generation < 500ms.
Error scenario: Test Swagger setup exception is caught, logged, and application continues running without crash.
